description: Add helper to parse pre-signed URLs from header values in tokens.ts
steps: Add ParsedPreSignedUrl interface with streamId, expires, and signature fields
Implement parsePreSignedUrl function to extract components from URL
Split HMAC validation: create validateHmac (signature only) separate from validatePreSignedUrl (signature + expiry)
Add unit tests for the new parsing and validation functions

description: Implement Use-Stream-URL header support in handleCreateStream
steps: Check for Use-Stream-URL header in incoming requests
Parse and validate HMAC (ignoring expiry) when header is present
Verify stream exists via HEAD request to durable streams server
Check stream is not closed (can still accept writes)
Skip stream creation (no PUT) when reusing stream
Pipe upstream response to existing stream via POST (append)
Return 200 OK with fresh signed URL in Location header for reuse
Return appropriate error codes: 400 (malformed), 401 (invalid HMAC), 404 (not found), 409 (closed)

description: Implement X-Stream-TTL header support for configurable URL expiry
steps: Parse X-Stream-TTL header value as seconds
Pass TTL value to URL generation functions
Default to 7 days (604800 seconds) when not specified
Support infinite TTL (0 or not present when server default is infinite)

description: Create POST /v1/proxy/renew endpoint for URL renewal
steps: Create new file renew-stream.ts with handleRenewStream handler
Parse Use-Stream-URL header and validate HMAC (ignore expiry)
Parse Upstream-URL header (the renewUrl)
Forward request to renewUrl with client's auth headers
On upstream 2xx: generate fresh signed URL, return 200 with Location
On upstream 4xx: return 401 Unauthorized
On non-existent stream: return 404 Not Found
Register endpoint in proxy-handler.ts

description: Update read-stream.ts to return structured error for expired URLs
steps: Distinguish between expired-but-valid-HMAC and invalid-HMAC errors
Return JSON response with renewable:true for expired but valid HMAC
Include error, message, renewable, and streamId fields in response

description: Add session credential storage functions to client storage.ts
steps: Add SessionCredentials interface with streamUrl and streamId fields
Implement createSessionStorageKey function
Implement saveSessionCredentials function
Implement loadSessionCredentials function
Implement removeSessionCredentials function

description: Update client types.ts with new options
steps: Add sessionId option to DurableFetchOptions
Add getSessionId callback option to DurableFetchOptions
Add ttl option to DurableFetchOptions
Add renewUrl option to DurableFetchOptions
Add sessionId option to DurableFetchRequestOptions

description: Implement session-based stream reuse in durable-fetch.ts
steps: Accept sessionId, getSessionId, ttl, and renewUrl options
Send X-Stream-TTL header when ttl is configured
Resolve effective sessionId per request (priority: request > getSessionId > options)
Check requestId credentials first (existing resume path)
Check sessionId credentials if no requestId credentials
Add Use-Stream-URL header when session credentials exist
Store/update session credentials after successful response
Store request credentials with current offset as starting point

description: Implement 409 Conflict handling for closed streams
steps: Detect 409 Conflict response from server
Clear session credentials on 409
Retry request without Use-Stream-URL header to create new stream

description: Implement client auto-renewal for expired read URLs
steps: Intercept 401 responses on reads
Check for renewable:true in response body
If renewUrl configured: POST to /v1/proxy/renew with expired URL and renewUrl
Update stored credentials with fresh URL on success
Retry the read transparently
Surface error to caller if renewUrl not configured or renewal fails
Export SessionCredentials type

description: Add stream-reuse.test.ts for stream reuse functionality
steps: Test first request creates stream and stores session credentials
Test second request with same sessionId appends to stream (200 OK)
Test fresh signed URL returned on every 200 and 201
Test X-Stream-TTL respected in generated URLs
Test expired HMAC accepted on write path when upstream returns 2xx
Test expired HMAC rejected on read path with renewable:true
Test invalid HMAC rejected on all paths
Test non-existent stream returns 404
Test closed stream returns 409, client creates new stream
Test resume (requestId) works within session stream
Test mixed session/non-session requests
Test session override per-request
Test getSessionId derivation
Test /v1/proxy/renew success: valid HMAC + upstream 2xx returns fresh URL
Test /v1/proxy/renew failure: valid HMAC + upstream 4xx returns 401
Test /v1/proxy/renew failure: invalid HMAC returns 401
Test /v1/proxy/renew with non-existent stream returns 404
Test client auto-renewal with renewUrl configured
Test client error surfacing without renewUrl configured
Test infinite TTL: URL never expires on reads

description: Fix session credential persistence after auto-renewal
steps: In durable-fetch.ts:490-507, save renewed credentials to storage after successful renewal
Update both session credentials and request credentials with the renewed URL
Verify subsequent reads use the fresh URL without needing to renew again

description: Add missing CORS headers for Use-Stream-URL and X-Stream-TTL
steps: In proxy-handler.ts, add Use-Stream-URL to Access-Control-Allow-Headers
In proxy-handler.ts, add X-Stream-TTL to Access-Control-Allow-Headers
Verify browser clients can send these headers in cross-origin requests

description: Fix incomplete 404 test for non-existent stream in stream-reuse.test.ts
steps: Replace expect(true).toBe(true) placeholder with actual test logic
Generate a valid signed URL for a non-existent stream using generatePreSignedUrl
Follow the pattern from renew-stream.test.ts:160-181 for correct approach
Verify 404 response is returned for non-existent stream with valid HMAC

description: Fix streamExists function to handle server errors correctly
steps: In renew-stream.ts:50-51, change return logic from 'ok || status !== 404' to just 'ok'
Ensure 500 errors do not falsely indicate stream exists
Only return true when HEAD response is successful (2xx)

description: Add retry limit to auto-renewal to prevent infinite loops
steps: Add retryCount parameter to readFromStream function with default 0
Set maximum retry limit (e.g., 1 retry)
Increment retry count on each recursive call
Return error instead of retrying when limit exceeded

description: Add integration test for full client auto-renewal flow
steps: Write test that exercises: client read -> 401 with renewable:true -> /v1/proxy/renew call -> retry read with fresh URL
Verify credentials are persisted after renewal
Verify subsequent reads use fresh URL without renewal
